evals.prompt: Generate a kagent agent for Flux CD. Here are the details: cncf_project_url: https://fluxcd.io, github_repo_url: https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2, default_enabled: true
evals.expected_output: Complete helm chart package for flux-agent including Chart-template.yaml with ${VERSION}, values.yaml with exactly three top-level keys, templates/agent.yaml with full system message grounded in Flux CRDs (Kustomization, HelmRelease, GitRepository etc.), templates/rbac.yaml for fluxcd.io API group, and Phase 4 integration into helm/kagent/Chart-template.yaml, helm/kagent/values.yaml, and Makefile helm-agents target

assertions.name: correct_slug
assertions.description: Uses slug 'flux' and agent name 'flux-agent'

assertions.name: version_placeholder
assertions.description: Chart-template.yaml uses literal ${VERSION} not a real semver

assertions.name: rbac_generated
assertions.description: Generates rbac.yaml since Flux introduces CRDs (kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io, helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io, etc.)

assertions.name: real_tool_names_only
assertions.description: All toolNames are from the kagent-tool-server catalogue — no invented names

assertions.name: makefile_integration
assertions.description: Phase 4 includes envsubst + helm package lines in helm-agents target with tab indentation

assertions.name: three_builtins_present
assertions.description: systemMessage includes all three builtin includes: kubernetes-context, tool-usage-best-practices, safety-guardrails

assertions.name: a2a_skills_count
assertions.description: Agent has 3-5 a2aConfig.skills entries, each with 3-5 examples

evals.prompt: Create a kagent agent for Argo CD. cncf_project_url: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io, github_repo_url: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd, default_enabled: false
evals.expected_output: Complete helm chart package for argocd-agent with default_enabled set to false in values.yaml. Should include CRD-based RBAC for argoproj.io API group, system message covering Application sync, AppProject management, rollback, health status. Phase 4 wires into main chart with enabled: false so users must opt-in with --set.

assertions.name: default_enabled_false
assertions.description: helm/kagent/values.yaml shows 'enabled: false' for argocd-agent

assertions.name: correct_api_group_in_rbac
assertions.description: rbac.yaml targets argoproj.io API group

assertions.name: helm_tools_included
assertions.description: Includes helm_ tools since Argo CD manages Helm releases

assertions.name: phase4_complete
assertions.description: All three Phase 4 targets updated: Chart-template.yaml, values.yaml, Makefile

evals.prompt: I want to generate a kagent agent for cert-manager. cncf_project_url: https://cert-manager.io, github_repo_url: https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager, default_enabled: true
evals.expected_output: Agent slug 'cert-manager', agent name 'cert-manager-agent'. rbac.yaml for cert-manager.io API group covering Certificate, CertificateRequest, Issuer, ClusterIssuer, Order, Challenge resources. System message focuses on certificate lifecycle, renewal troubleshooting, issuer configuration (ACME/Let's Encrypt, self-signed, CA), and secret management.

assertions.name: slug_with_hyphen
assertions.description: Correctly uses kebab-case slug 'cert-manager' and handles it in all file paths

assertions.name: cert_manager_crds_in_rbac
assertions.description: rbac.yaml covers cert-manager.io CRDs: certificates, issuers, clusterissuers, certificaterequests

assertions.name: rbac_role_naming
assertions.description: ClusterRole named cert-manager-role (not cert-manager-agent-role)

assertions.name: system_message_grounded
assertions.description: System message references actual cert-manager CRD kinds and operational tasks from Phase 1 research

evals.prompt: Make a kagent agent for Prometheus. Project URL: https://prometheus.io, GitHub: https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus, enabled by default: yes
evals.expected_output: Should stop and ask for clarification — 'yes' is not a valid value for default_enabled. Must ask user to provide 'true' or 'false' before proceeding.

assertions.name: stops_for_invalid_argument
assertions.description: Does NOT proceed to Phase 1 — asks user to clarify default_enabled value

assertions.name: explains_valid_values
assertions.description: Tells user the valid values are 'true' or 'false'

evals.prompt: Create a kagent agent for OpenTelemetry Collector. cncf_project_url: https://opentelemetry.io, github_repo_url: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector, default_enabled: true
evals.expected_output: Agent for opentelemetry-collector (or otel-collector slug). Should include RBAC for opentelemetry.io API group (OpenTelemetryCollector, Instrumentation CRDs from the operator). System message covers pipeline configuration, receiver/processor/exporter setup, troubleshooting collector pods, and Kubernetes resource instrumentation.

assertions.name: reasonable_slug
assertions.description: Uses a sensible kebab-case slug (otel-collector or opentelemetry-collector)

assertions.name: rbac_for_otel_crds
assertions.description: Generates rbac.yaml for opentelemetry.io API group

assertions.name: phase4_makefile
assertions.description: Makefile helm-agents target updated with correct tab-indented envsubst + helm package lines

evals.prompt: Generate a kagent agent for Crossplane. cncf_project_url: https://www.crossplane.io, github_repo_url: https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane, default_enabled: true
evals.expected_output: Agent for Crossplane with RBAC covering crossplane.io and pkg.crossplane.io API groups (Provider, Configuration, Composite Resources, Claims). System message covers provider installation, composite resource definitions (XRDs), compositions, and troubleshooting claim binding.

assertions.name: multiple_api_groups_in_rbac
assertions.description: rbac.yaml covers multiple Crossplane API groups: crossplane.io, pkg.crossplane.io, apiextensions.crossplane.io

assertions.name: no_invented_tools
assertions.description: Does not invent crossplane_* tool names — uses only real k8s_ and helm_ tools from catalogue

assertions.name: quality_checklist_passed
assertions.description: All 20 quality checklist items verified before output presented

evals.prompt: Create a kagent agent for Istio. cncf_project_url: https://istio.io, github_repo_url: https://github.com/istio/istio, default_enabled: true
evals.expected_output: Istio agent with RBAC for networking.istio.io, security.istio.io API groups. System message covers VirtualService, DestinationRule, Gateway management, mTLS, traffic management, and observability. Should check whether istio_* tools exist in catalogue before including them — if not, use only k8s_ tools.

assertions.name: checks_tool_catalogue
assertions.description: Only includes tools that actually exist in kagent-tool-server (istio_* tools if real, otherwise k8s_ only)

assertions.name: istio_crds_in_rbac
assertions.description: rbac.yaml covers networking.istio.io and security.istio.io API groups

assertions.name: promptTemplate_before_modelConfig
assertions.description: In agent.yaml declarative block, promptTemplate appears before modelConfig

evals.prompt: I want to generate a kagent agent but I only have the GitHub URL: https://github.com/cilium/cilium. I don't have the project URL.
evals.expected_output: Should stop and ask for the missing cncf_project_url and default_enabled values before proceeding. Should list all three required arguments with their validation rules and examples.

assertions.name: stops_for_missing_args
assertions.description: Does not proceed to Phase 1 with incomplete arguments

assertions.name: lists_all_missing
assertions.description: Identifies both cncf_project_url and default_enabled as missing

assertions.name: provides_format_guidance
assertions.description: Shows the expected format for each missing argument
